mechanism: Freeze Round61 feedback path; apply innovation-consistency adaptation only to SCD xx/zz subpath

dataset.constraint_note: Use exactly the same fixed noisy dataset/noise strength/seed as Round65 mainline

clean_win_gate: mean<0, max<=0, dKg_xx<0 and no hard-protected regression vs Round61

round66_candidates.name: r66_scd_xxzz_consis_mild_f90
round66_candidates.description: Consistency-adaptive SCD only on xx/zz with conservative floor, keeping Round61 feedback fully frozen.
round66_candidates.rationale: Most conservative new-direction probe: only allow tiny SCD modulation on xx/zz while preserving Round61 feedback body unchanged.

round66_candidates.name: r66_scd_xxzz_consis_bal_f82
round66_candidates.description: Balanced xx/zz-only adaptive SCD with moderate floor and slope.
round66_candidates.rationale: Test whether moderate SCD-only adaptation can recover dKg_zz without leaking regressions to yy/Ka_xx-protected paths.

round66_candidates.name: r66_scd_xxzz_consis_slowema_f85
round66_candidates.description: xx/zz-only adaptive SCD with slower consistency EMA and higher floor.
round66_candidates.rationale: Reduce gate jitter to keep mechanism deterministic/interpretable and avoid overreacting to transient innovation fluctuations.

round66_candidates.name: r66_scd_xxzz_consis_push_f75
round66_candidates.description: More aggressive xx/zz-only adaptive SCD gate to stress-test the narrow mechanism limit.
round66_candidates.rationale: Probe upper bound of narrow SCD-only adaptation strength to check whether a stronger xx/zz correction can beat Round61 cleanly.
